column1: The process by which the primitive node induces the overlying ectoderm to thicken into a plate is called _____.
On which day of human development does the neural plate first form?

The neural plate consists of a thick plate of which specific cell type?
In which direction does the differentiation of the neural plate typically proceed?
The lateral edges of the neural plate that detach during tube formation are called _____ cells.
Which specific process converts the neural plate into a hollow neural tube?
By day 28, the brain is divided into which three primary vesicles?
The prosencephalon (forebrain) subdivides into which two secondary vesicles during the fifth week?
The rhombencephalon (hindbrain) subdivides into which two secondary vesicles?
How many secondary brain vesicles exist in total by the end of the fifth week?
What are the transient, repetitive segments that partition the developing hindbrain called?
The mesencephalic flexure, located at the midbrain, is directed in which anatomical direction?

Where is the cervical flexure located in the developing neural tube?
column2: Near the juncture between the myelencephalon and the spinal cord

column1: Within the differentiating neural tube, in which layer does mitosis of neuroepithelial cells occur?
Which layer of the developing neural tube contains the neuronal cell bodies and becomes gray matter?
The marginal layer of the central nervous system contains nerve fibers and becomes _____ matter.
Which specialized glial cells serve as the physical scaffolding for migrating neurons during brain development?
Which cells are responsible for forming myelin sheaths in the central nervous system?
Schwann cells, which form myelin sheaths in the peripheral nervous system, are derived from _____.
Nerve cell bodies in the peripheral nervous system (PNS) aggregate into structures called _____.
What is the function of satellite cells in the peripheral nervous system?
column2: They surround and support nerve cell bodies within ganglia

column1: Which specific cells line the central canal of the spinal cord and the ventricles of the brain?
On which day of human development does the heart typically begin to beat?

The cardiac primordium forms within which embryonic tissue layer?
What name is given to the cardiac progenitor region at the cranial end of the embryonic disc?
Which process is responsible for the formation of the lateral endocardial tubes?
The single primary heart tube is formed by the fusion of endocardial tubes due to which embryonic process?
List the three basic layers of the primary heart tube from internal to external.
column2: Endocardial tube, cardiac jelly, and myocardium

column1: The extracellular matrix situated between the endocardium and myocardium in the early heart is the _____.

During heart looping, the primitive atrium is displaced in which directions?

The proepicardial organ is located in the region of which heart tube segment?

column2: The left sinus horn (of the sinus venosus)

column1: Which layer of the adult heart is histologically equivalent to the tunica intima of blood vessels?
The tunica media of a blood vessel is functionally comparable to which layer of the heart?
What are the three layers of the wall of a typical artery or vein?
column2: Tunica intima, tunica media, and tunica adventitia

column1: The de novo formation of blood vessels from endothelial precursor cells is called _____.
The budding and sprouting of new vessels from existing endothelial cords is known as _____.
What are the cell aggregations in the yolk sac wall that give rise to both blood and endothelial lineages?
Clusters of hemangioblasts and blood cells within the yolk sac are called _____.
When does blood vessel formation begin in the human yolk sac?
In the central nervous system, gray matter is primarily composed of _____.
What type of epithelium characterizes the early neural plate?
Which structure connects the primitive atrium to the primitive left ventricle in the heart tube?
Which brain vesicle gives rise to the cerebral hemispheres?
The thalamus and hypothalamus develop from which secondary brain vesicle?
Which secondary vesicle develops into the pons and cerebellum?

Neural crest cells undergo which specific cellular transformation to begin migration?
Which neuropore typically closes first during human neurulation?
On which day of development does the caudal neuropore typically close?
The dorsal portion of the telencephalon that forms the cerebral cortex is called the _____.
What is the fate of the primitive right ventricle during heart development?
column2: It contributes to the definitive right ventricle

column1: Which transcription factor is predominantly expressed in the pacemaker and conducting system of the developing heart?
The intraembryonic source of definitive hematopoietic stem cells is the _____ region.
What is the process called where existing vessels split to generate additional vessels?
Which embryonic system drains the head, neck, and body wall during early development?
The umbilical veins carry which type of blood from the placenta to the embryo?
Which heart horn receives all systemic venous blood after remodeling is complete?
column2: Right sinus horn (via superior and inferior caval veins)

column1: In the developing heart, the division of the outflow tract creates the ascending aorta and the _____.

Which structure lies at the midline just deep to the neural plate and aids in its induction?

In the adult heart, what is the layer equivalent to the tunica adventitia of a blood vessel?
The process where the neural plate narrows transversely and lengthens longitudinally is called _____.

The major shift of systemic venous return to the right side of the heart involves the remodeling of which system?

column2: It folds the metencephalon back against the myelencephalon

column1: Cells in the adult brain that retain the ability to divide and differentiate into neurons are called _____.

The space within the heart tube before septation is initially a _____ tube.

column2: On either side of the notochord

column1: What term is used for endothelial precursor cells derived from splanchnic mesoderm?
